isolation.sharedState: none - read-only file checks and CLI commands

assertions.title: Harness API documentation exists and is accurate

steps.action: Check sections for all JSON output shapes
steps.expected: Sections for Search, Index Search, Rewrite Plan, Apply+Verify, GPU Sidecar
steps.observed: All 5 sections found: ## Search JSON, ## Index Search JSON, ## Rewrite Plan JSON, ## Apply + Verify JSON, ## GPU Sidecar JSON

steps.expected: Field names, types, when emitted, flags documented
steps.observed: Each section contains tables with Field, Type, Notes columns. Common envelope fields documented. Rust vs Python field differences (line vs line_number) documented.

assertions.title: Example artifacts exist for every JSON shape

steps.expected: At least 5 files covering all shapes
steps.observed: 5 files found: gpu_sidecar_search.json (620B), index_search.json (552B), rewrite_apply_verify.json (1559B), rewrite_plan.json (1249B), search.json (519B)

steps.expected: (a) search, (b) index search, (c) rewrite plan, (d) apply+verify, (e) GPU sidecar
steps.observed: All 5 shapes covered: search.json, index_search.json, rewrite_plan.json, rewrite_apply_verify.json, gpu_sidecar_search.json

assertions.title: Example artifacts are valid single-document JSON

steps.action: Run python -c "import json, pathlib; [json.loads(p.read_text()) for p in pathlib.Path('docs/examples').glob('*.json')]"
steps.observed: Exit code 0. Output: 'Parsed 5 JSON files successfully'. No trailing commas, comments, concatenated objects, or JSON Lines detected.

assertions.title: Example artifacts contain realistic non-trivial data

steps.action: Parse each JSON file and check total_matches/total_edits and array lengths
steps.observed: gpu_sidecar_search.json: total=2, array_len=2, non_empty=True; index_search.json: total=2, array_len=2, non_empty=True; rewrite_apply_verify.json: total=2, array_len=2, non_empty=True; rewrite_plan.json: total=2, array_len=2, non_empty=True; search.json: total=2, array_len=2, non_empty=True

steps.expected: Non-empty strings, valid line numbers, real file paths
steps.observed: All match objects contain real file paths, line numbers >= 1, non-empty text fields (e.g., 'ERROR database failed', 'def add(x, y): return x + y')

assertions.title: Rewrite --diff output is valid unified diff format

steps.action: Create test fixture: bench_data/val_test_diff/fixture.py with two def lines
steps.observed: Fixture created with content: def add(x, y): return x + y\ndef mul(a, b): return a * b

steps.action: Record file mtime before running tg.exe
Run tg.exe run --lang python --rewrite 'lambda $$$ARGS: $EXPR' --diff 'def $F($$$ARGS): return $EXPR' fixture.py
steps.expected: Exit code 0, unified diff format output
steps.observed: Exit code 0. Output contains --- a/path, +++ b/path, @@ -1,2 +1,2 @@ hunk header, - removal lines, + addition lines
mtime after: 2026-03-16T04:32:13.7337951Z — identical. File content unchanged.

summary: Tested 5 assertions: 5 passed, 0 failed, 0 blocked. All docs/examples artifacts are present, valid JSON, contain realistic data. The harness_api.md documentation covers all 5 JSON output shapes with field-level detail. The --diff output produces valid unified diff format without modifying files.
